back_translation: Man, who is born of woman, has but few days and is full of trouble.
He springs up like a flower and then withers; he passes away like a fleeting shadow that cannot endure.
Do You open Your eyes to watch one like this, and bring him into judgment with You?
Who can bring something clean out of something unclean? No one!
Since his days are determined and the number of his months is with You, You have set a limit he cannot pass.
Look away from him and let him rest, until he can enjoy his day like a hired hand.
For there is hope for a tree: if it is cut down, it will sprout again, and its tender shoots will not fail.
Though its roots grow old in the ground and its stump dies in the soil,
at the mere scent of water it will bud and put forth branches like a young plant.
But a man dies and is laid low; he breathes his last, and where is he?
As water vanishes from the sea and a river dries up,
so a man lies down and does not rise again; until the heavens are no more, he will not awake or be roused from sleep.
Oh, that You would hide me in Sheol and conceal me until Your anger passes, that You would appoint a time for me and then remember me!
When a man dies, will he live again? All the days of my hard service I will wait, until my renewal comes.
You will call, and I will answer You; You will long for the work of Your hands.
For then You would count my steps, but would not keep watch over my sin.
My transgression would be sealed up in a bag, and You would cover over my iniquity.
But as a mountain collapses and erodes away, and a rock is moved from its place,
as water wears away the stones and torrents wash away the soil, so You destroy the hope of man.
You overpower him forever, and he passes on; You change his face and send him away.
If his sons receive honor, he does not know it; if they are brought low, he is not aware.
He feels only the pain of his own body, and mourns only for himself.
